about.title: About the book
about.description: Shepherd Down the Meadows narrates the compelling journey of Jamal Durham, a gifted orphan and shepherd, as he navigates the challenges of life in his idyllic village. Following the loss of his parents at a young age, Jamal's resilience shines through as he transforms the land of his birth into a source of inspiration and fulfilment. The story unfolds against the backdrop of a serene river that nourishes both his community and his dreams.

poemReveal.title: Poem Spotlight
poemReveal.intro: A dedicated immersive section for the current poem. Open it and let the rhythm unfold smoothly.
lines: POEM: I will always remember
I remember a lazy wind whistling and buffeting my universe,
my cosy place, my sweet, sweet home.
I remember being cloistered in a makeshift abode
cladded with bits of cracking pallets hanging higgledy-piggledy.
I remember a poor drowning man clutching at straws,
huddling against false hopes for survival.
I remember when they pelted his skinny and defenceless
body with stones and thorny sticks.
I remember him meandering in windy towns,
sneaking through hounds of winter and thwarting pitfalls.
I remember him sending rafts of SOS messages
to call out rubbernecks and onlookers sailing by.
I remember him being the victim of unfortunate circumstances,
hidden from light not to see the day heaving in sight.
I also remember him down in the dumps, in dire straits,
falling back over scattered bread crumbs.
I remember them blowing a crackling fire with glowing embers
to rekindle the shabby flames of despair.
I remember them plastering him with myriads of exotic funny names,
stereotypes of a spurned outcast in a babble of sounds.
I remember him trying to rise again in tattered rags
wrapped around his slim silhouette riddled with bullets.
I remember him sheltering from the striking sunbeams
and staying away from the unkempt appearance of devilish faces.
I remember him holding back his tears, his fear and despair
while being pestered by ranting perpetrators.
I remember the storyteller narrating the feats of Jack the ripper,
the famous unidentified serial killer under his leather apron.
So always, remember the shadows of death lingering on and on
to quench their everlasting thirst for power.
POEM: Damned Survivors
A vital quest for revival,
Revival of a paradise lost,
For blurred souls dwindling at any cost,
And waning in an ultimate time of trial.
So beset with doubts,
Gathered in an artifice of fears,
Artifice of vain feats and tears,
When jostled and hustled about.
With drab and smeared hearts,
Seek for snatches of grounds,
To dodge riddling darts,
Between heaven and earth for no grounds.
With an awkward and spooky silence,
Brooding over a filthy scene of pestilence,
Of innumerable flow of blind souls,
Remaining unsteady in fouls.
A flow of fettered people in a row,
People sloping down to the sea,
After a surge and undertow,
Gazed at far stranded whales in plea.
Tramping and scrambling,
All along seashores,
Thrusting aside shingles and pebbles,
Tried desperately to crack riddles,
When a lazy and hazy wind was wailing.
Time seemed to be a healer,
Healer in their sluggish mind,
Real victims of a storyteller,
They have ultimately turned blind.
Driven into hell,
They were under a ghost's spell,
Within a sorry plight,
Play havoc with their attempts to fight.
Their meandering talks,
All through a babble of sounds,
Like fowls among mobs of hounds,
They uprose to vanquish in stalk.
A fall shortfall at their footfall,
A call that was not at all a recall,
For old times' sake,
Help them to shake.
How pride is good for extol,
To be praised after such a toll,
With gestures, with hands,
With legs thrown about in lands.
Truth, so much truth,
As a dazzling crystalline gleam,
Shining over their murky past
And in such metaphors they dream.
Cloistered blatantly in reclusion,
Their guts and courage braced up,
Just raised to the bait and hoisted up,
Swang and trod into huge profusion.
In an unevenly breath,
Heaving throbbing hearts,
Hearts filled with tears,
Tears shed on a barren soil.
Macoumba DIENG
Novelist/Poet

author.title: About the author
author.lead: A great literature enthusiast, Macoumba Dieng has established himself in a literary landscape that reflects human society in all its complexity. His philosophy and writing technique now allow him to evolve in this fascinating universe. As a novelist, he explores a narrative genre based on historical facts with a relatively fictional tone, featuring dynamic characters operating within a precise time frame.
author.bio: Through his conceptual writing technique, Dieng prefers to transport his readership through a series of spectacular outcomes and endless twists, all supported by a beautifully structured plot.
author.bio2: The author himself is passionate about literary research and is also an ardent poet, as evidenced by a collection of poems published in several magazines.
author.bio3: His narratives, which joyfully express his worldview, carry an emotional foundation with strong aesthetic character, delicately transposed into his different works.

items.item3: Voted most popular writer of the year

profile.name: Macoumba Dieng
profile.headline: Operations Manager - Trade, Investment and Market Development
profile.summary: Senior profile with multi-country experience in operations leadership, international trade advisory, investment facilitation and distribution strategy.
highlights: Operations management and supply chain optimization
Embassy-level advisory in trade and investment
Market intelligence, business development and strategic reporting

items.company: Societe Internationale de Distribution de Fruits (SIDF), Dakar
items.summary: Oversees daily operations, quality control, logistics, budget forecasting and team management to ensure reliable service delivery.

items.company: Department of International Trade, UK Embassy in Dakar
items.summary: Promoted UK trade in Senegal, facilitated investment missions, coordinated with government and private institutions, and produced market intelligence for exporters and investors.

items.company: Economic Office, South African Embassy in Dakar
items.summary: Led trade promotion initiatives, organized missions and trade events, coordinated partnerships and delivered bilingual support in strategic business engagements.

items.role: International Distribution Manager / Head of Trade Section
items.company: Wrigley Confectionery Company / National Chamber of Crafts, Senegal
items.summary: Managed national distribution strategy, channel development, reporting and institutional trade programs at national and international levels.
